fixedSinceLastReview: vibeStreak prop now correctly passed to Office component
workers.json roster key present — filter bar functional
stuck detection uses startedAt instead of .started
stillOpenFromReview7: ProgressBar ETA uses updated_at instead of startedAt (HIGH) — still present
React.memo on WorkerCard defeated by new activityLog array ref (HIGH) — still present

suggestions.title: Office.css — conference-table CSS block has broken brace structure
suggestions.description: In Office.css, the .conference-table rule opens with '{' but the .office-aisle block is INSIDE it — the brace is never properly closed before .office-aisle starts. This means all CSS after .conference-table { is nested incorrectly. The browser may recover, but rules after this point may not apply as intended. The .office-aisle block and everything after it are technically inside .conference-table's rule body. Fix: close the .conference-table block properly before .office-aisle.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast timer leak — STILL UNFIXED from review #7
suggestions.description: ShortcutToast dismiss() (App.jsx): when gotIt=true, timerRef.current is assigned a 500ms timeout, then IMMEDIATELY overwritten with a 900ms timeout. The 500ms timer is orphaned. Fix: chain with a single setTimeout or use two refs.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar ETA bug — STILL UNFIXED from review #7
suggestions.description: ProgressBar uses worker.updated_at (which advances every 30s poll) instead of worker.startedAt for ETA calculation. This makes ETAs wildly inaccurate. Fix: pass startedAt to ProgressBar.

suggestions.title: React.memo defeated — STILL UNFIXED from review #7
suggestions.description: syncFromGitHub creates a new activityLog array ref every 30s poll. This array is passed to every WorkerCard, defeating React.memo. ALL cards re-render every poll. Fix: useMemo for per-worker activity slices or useRef for stable log reference.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline reads localStorage BEFORE parent useEffect writes — always one entry behind
suggestions.description: VibeSparkline's useEffect reads VIBE_HISTORY_KEY from localStorage, but the parent App useEffect that WRITES to localStorage runs in the same render cycle. React doesn't guarantee sibling/child effect order. The sparkline is always one data point behind. Fix: lift vibe history state to App and pass it as a prop.

suggestions.title: Office floor characters inaccessible via keyboard and touch
suggestions.description: Character hover cards only show on mouseenter. Keyboard users (Tab) and touch/mobile users can never see worker task details or status in the Office view. The tabIndex={0} is set but no focus-visible styles exist. Fix: show hover card on :focus-visible too, add touch-friendly tap interaction.

suggestions.title: completionToast uses 12+ inline styles — unthemeable
suggestions.description: The completion toast (App.jsx) is rendered with a massive inline style object including colors, padding, border-radius, etc. This makes it impossible to theme with CSS variables and inconsistent with the rest of the app. Fix: extract to a CSS class.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated in Office.jsx ghost bubble
suggestions.description: The relative time calculation in the ghost OOO bubble (Office.jsx Character component) is a copy-paste of getRelativeTime from App.jsx. Fix: extract to a shared utility in utils/.

suggestions.title: StatsBar stuck-worker click uses document.querySelector — breaks on Office tab
suggestions.description: StatsBar's stuck worker card uses document.querySelector to scroll to a worker card by data-worker-name. This only works on the Dashboard tab. If the user is on Office tab, the click does nothing. Fix: use handleWorkerClick pattern (switch tab first).

suggestions.title: 19 ambient particle DOM nodes with permanent CSS animations
suggestions.description: 7 particles in Office.jsx + 12 in App.jsx = 19 permanently animating DOM elements. On low-end mobile devices, these cause jank. Consider: reduce count, use will-change sparingly, or pause when tab is hidden.

suggestions.title: Fading worker cards all get index={activeWorkers.length} — stagger animation broken
suggestions.description: In the workers grid, fading cards all receive the same index value, so their stagger animation delay is identical. Fix: use a counter that increments per fading card.
